rationale: Empty description; thin signal. Handling missing 'ok' field may touch wire protocol but insufficient detail to classify as nonconformance.
BSON decode failure on ARM; platform-specific C extension bug, not a spec rule violation.
DBPointer is a deprecated BSON type; oversight in Python 3 port of an obscure legacy type, not a current spec requirement.
Bug in SON manipulation behavior when manipulate=True; internal PyMongo feature not governed by a cross-driver spec.
Invalid BSON input causes parser crash; robustness/error-handling issue, no specific spec rule violated.
Internal refactor of type-checking in cursor; no spec behavior change, purely code quality improvement.
Write concern spec requires raising TimeoutError on wtimeout; driver raises generic OperationFailure instead.
Wrapping internal BSON decode exceptions in InvalidBSON; error-handling improvement, no specific spec rule contradicted.
Adding error detail attribute to OperationFailure; API enhancement, no cross-driver spec requirement violated.
Race condition in lazy connection with multiple threads; concurrency/implementation bug, not a spec rule violation.
Celery/fork-safety issue with monitor thread recreation; implementation/platform concern, not a spec requirement.
Adding a warning when command routed to primary despite non-primary read preference; UX improvement, not a spec violation.
Python dict key ordering is a Python language behavior; not a MongoDB spec issue. C# mention is user context only.
Assertion failure on SSL socket disconnect; implementation crash, no spec rule contradicted.
AttributeError NoneType in __schedule_refresh; internal implementation crash, not a spec deviation.
Replica set member verification bug; NODE-787 related link is a parallel fix, not a cross-driver inconsistency. SDAM coverage unclear without specific violated rule.
AssertionError during primary election; internal concurrency crash, no specific spec rule violated.
Adding 'multi' option to remove(); proactive spec-rollout improvement (DRIVERS-128), not a nonconformance bug.
MinKey/MaxKey comparison ordering; BSON spec does not define Python comparison operators, so not a spec rule violation.
Thundering herd / reconnect synchronization in mongos HA; internal concurrency issue, no spec rule violated.
GridFS.list() returns None for nameless files after get_last_version; internal state bug, GridFS spec does not define this edge case.
Timestamp ordering operators not implemented; BSON spec does not define Python comparison semantics, so not a spec violation.
Split-brain monitoring drops isolated members then never re-monitors them; implementation issue, no specific SDAM rule cited.
GridFS file truncation on Windows Python 2.7; platform-specific binary mode bug, not a spec rule violation.
Client should raise error when all addressed hosts are unreachable; connection behavior, but no specific SDAM rule precisely named.
json_util.dumps field ordering for SON objects; serialization ergonomic improvement, no spec rule.
GridFS.put() memory issue with very large files; performance/implementation improvement, not a spec rule violation.
PCREs unsupported by Python raise error on decode; error-handling improvement, BSON spec does not define Python regex compilation behavior.
Performance improvement to alive() method; no spec behavior change.
Feature request to add cursor-based GridFS file listing; API addition, not a spec nonconformance.
read(0) returns str instead of bytes in Python 3; Python 3 porting bug in GridFS, no cross-driver spec rule violated.
Feature request for GridFS version count API; no spec requirement violated.
Driver should reject _id keys starting with $; validation hardening, BSON spec does not explicitly require driver-side rejection.
Documentation inconsistency for max_pool_size default value; docs bug only.
User confusion about sort+distinct interaction; user error or query semantics question, not a spec violation.
Intermittent test failure in Jenkins; CI flakiness, not a spec behavior issue.
Gevent 1.0 epoll hang due to DNS thread pool interaction; third-party library compatibility issue, not a spec violation.
Kerberos/GSSAPI auth failure with Red Hat python-kerberos package; platform-specific third-party library bug, not a spec rule violation.
Support (str, dict) pairs in sort for $meta textScore; API ergonomics enhancement, DRIVERS-137 proactive rollout.
Unexpected $push $sort array ordering; likely user misunderstanding of server behavior, no spec rule named.
Aggregate cursor getMore fails on non-PRIMARY secondary; cursor must track originating server per spec.
Adding parallelCollectionScan helper per DRIVERS-138; new feature addition, driver not previously violating any spec.
Cursor spec requires tracking killed state on CursorNotFound; driver fails to set __killed flag causing wrong alive() and spurious OP_KILL_CURSORS.
Missing sslPEMKeyPassword option in driver; TLS configuration API gap, not a spec rule violation.
Documentation update for fsync write concern behavior; docs-only improvement, no spec nonconformance.
add_user role assignment bug on MongoDB 2.6; driver API behavior change across server versions, no explicit cross-driver spec rule violated.
Bulk API spec requires omitting nModified from legacy server results; driver includes it, contradicting DRIVERS-143/145 spec requirement.
Django integration documentation update; docs only, no spec behavior.
maxBsonObjectSize reported as 0 during primary election; race condition in connection state, implementation bug not tied to a specific spec rule.
Lazily-connected MongoClient fails update/remove with 'not master'; driver must ensure connection before all write operations per spec.
